commands.repair_reason: No repair lane is needed because the PR has no discrete actionable defect; the remaining action is normal maintainer review and merge/check gating.; Cleared: Cleared: the diff keeps signature and payload validation before HTTP 200 and does not add dependencies, permissions, secrets handling, package-resolution changes, or new network/code-execution surfaces. (sha=4e7900d126e1da77ef28cb6812587f19fc0c5c62)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes LINE Node, Express, and monitor webhook paths to send HTTP 200 after validation, process events asynchronously with runtime error logging, update LINE ACK metadata/tests, and add a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. A signed LINE POST with events and a pending `handleWebhook` or `onEvents` promise on current main keeps the HTTP 200 unsent because the ACK occurs after the awaited agent-processing call in the Node, Express, and monitor paths.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed because the PR has no discrete actionable defect; the remaining action is normal maintainer review and merge/check gating.

target.title: fix(line): respond 200 to webhook before agent processing (#65375)
target.author: perng

commands.author: perng
commands.command: findings addressed in `8add1a3b19`: > [p2] update line receive ack metadata — `extensions/line/src/webhook-node.ts:111` … `linemessageadapter.message.receive.defaultackpolicy` and its contract test still advertise `after_agent_dispatch`. good catch — fixed. - [extensions/line/src/outbound.ts:424](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/8add1a3b19/extensions/line/src/outbound.ts#l424): `defaultackpolicy` flipped from `after_agent_dispatch` to `after_receive_record` to match the runtime change. - `supportedackpolicies` keeps both values: `after_receive_record` is the new default, and `after_agent_dispatch` stays in the supported set since the adapter's contract still allows callers that need post-dispatch ack timing (no runtime path uses it today, but the sdk contract permits it). - [extensions/line/src/channel.sendpayload.test.ts:539-565](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/8add1a3b19/extensions/line/src/channel.sendpayload.test.ts#l539-l565): contract test `declares receive ack policies for deferred line webhook acknowledgement` now asserts `defaultackpolicy === "after_receive_record"` and verifies both policies remain in `supportedackpolicies`. local: 258/258 line tests pass. `pnpm check:changed` green (typecheck, lint, sidecar guard, import cycles). re-review when ready: `@clawsweeper re-review`.
commands.freeform_prompt: findings addressed in `8add1a3b19`: > [P2] Update LINE receive ACK metadata — `extensions/line/src/webhook-node.ts:111` … `lineMessageAdapter.message.receive.defaultAckPolicy` and its contract test still advertise `after_agent_dispatch`. Good catch — fixed. - [extensions/line/src/outbound.ts:424](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/8add1a3b19/extensions/line/src/outbound.ts#L424): `defaultAckPolicy` flipped from `after_agent_dispatch` to `after_receive_record` to match the runtime change. - `supportedAckPolicies` keeps both values: `after_receive_record` is the new default, and `after_agent_dispatch` stays in the supported set since the adapter's contract still allows callers that need post-dispatch ack timing (no runtime path uses it today, but the SDK contract permits it). - [extensions/line/src/channel.sendPayload.test.ts:539-565](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/8add1a3b19/extensions/line/src/channel.sendPayload.test.ts#L539-L565): contract test `declares receive ack policies for deferred LINE webhook acknowledgement` now asserts `defaultAckPolicy === "after_receive_record"` and verifies both policies remain in `supportedAckPolicies`. Local: 258/258 LINE tests pass. `pnpm check:changed` green (typecheck, lint, sidecar guard, import cycles). Re-review when ready: `@clawsweeper re-review`.
